symptoms.symptom_name: Carpopedal spasm (inward-curling hands) with facial twitching

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carpopedal spasm with facial twitching is the hallmark of tetany from low ionised calcium.
Abnormal sustained movements in a neonate cannot exclude seizure without EEG/observation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spasm not abolished by passive limb restraint; stimulus-sensitive

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistence despite gentle restraint and stimulus sensitivity indicate pathological neuromuscular irritability.
Benign jitteriness characteristically settles when the limb is gently held; failure to settle argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Chvostek's sign (facial nerve tap elicits lip/cheek twitch)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chvostek's sign reflects heightened neuromuscular excitability of hypocalcaemia; clinician-elicited, not available in telehealth.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Ionised / serum calcium (blood gas or venous sample)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Misses the treatable cause of tetany; delays IV calcium replacement.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low measured calcium confirms hypocalcaemia as the cause of the tetany; hospital investigation only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient perioral cyanosis with breath-holding during spasm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Signals laryngospasm/apnoea risk during tetanic episodes — an immediate airway threat mandating escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor feeding and irritability since nursery discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific; consistent with an evolving metabolic disturbance but also with many neonatal illnesses.
Poor feeding and irritability are cardinal non-specific signs of neonatal sepsis and must be screened.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known unrepaired conotruncal heart defect (Tetralogy of Fallot)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: DiGeorge syndrome with acute symptomatic hypocalcaemic tetany
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Conotruncal defects are a core feature of the 22q11.2 deletion constellation and raise pre-test probability of parathyroid hypoplasia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: DiGeorge syndrome with acute symptomatic hypocalcaemic tetany
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palatal anomalies are part of the pharyngeal-pouch derivative defects of 22q11.2 deletion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: DiGeorge syndrome with acute symptomatic hypocalcaemic tetany
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Facial dysmorphism supports a syndromic diagnosis but is non-specific and carer-observed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Not receiving calcium or vitamin D supplementation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of calcium/vitamin D support leaves an at-risk neonate unprotected against hypocalcaemia.
Consistent with the known cardiac lesion; auscultation is clinician-only and unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible prolonged QT interval from hypocalcaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Misses hypocalcaemia-related arrhythmia risk during calcium replacement.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypocalcaemia prolongs the QT interval and predisposes to arrhythmia; hospital monitoring only.
Absence of fever lowers but does NOT exclude neonatal sepsis, which can be afebrile or hypothermic.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sustained carpopedal/facial spasm, failure to settle with restraint, positive Chvostek and transient cyanosis together strongly indicate pathological hypocalcaemic tetany, not benign jitteriness.
Conotruncal cardiac defect plus cleft palate plus dysmorphic facies raises pre-test probability of parathyroid hypoplasia and hence hypocalcaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms symptomatic hypocalcaemia as the treatable cause and guides IV calcium replacement — hospital, not telehealth.
Rapid exclusion of hypoglycaemia in any jittery/unwell neonate.
Detects hypocalcaemia-related QT prolongation/arrhythmia, compounded by the underlying cardiac lesion, during replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Sepsis screen (FBC, CRP, blood culture, consider LP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Poor feeding and irritability in a neonate mandate exclusion of sepsis even when afebrile.
Characterises the metabolic disturbance (hypoparathyroidism) and co-existing magnesium deficiency.
Confirms the underlying syndrome once the acute emergency is stabilised.
An acutely unwell neonate needs emergency stabilisation now, not a routine outpatient investigation; EEG must not delay 000.
